PAGE_TITLE: Disqus Alternative: Faster, Private, No Ads | FastComments
META_DESCRIPTION: FastComments is the Disqus alternative built for speed and privacy. One script under 30 kilobytes, no ads, no tracking, and built-in Disqus import. Trusted by <%- Number(Math.floor(usageStats.tenantCount / 100) * 100).toLocaleString() %>+ organizations.
HEAD_KEYWORDS: disqus alternative, fastcomments vs disqus, switch from disqus, disqus replacement, disqus alternative no ads, disqus alternative privacy
HERO_SUBHEAD: Disqus adds seconds of load time, dozens of requests, and advertising trackers to every page view. FastComments loads one script under 30 kilobytes, shows no ads, tracks no one, and imports your existing Disqus comments in minutes.
PILLARS_HEADING_SR: Six reasons to switch from Disqus
PERF_BODY: Page speed affects your search rankings, bounce rate, and reader experience. Google's Core Web Vitals penalize slow third-party scripts. Here is how the two platforms compare on the metrics that matter.
PERF_STAT_1_NOTE: One small script tag. No iframes to bootstrap, no ad network payload.
PERF_STAT_2_LABEL: Added page load time with Disqus
PERF_STAT_2_NOTE: Independent measurement of a live production site.
PERF_STAT_3_LABEL: Requests Disqus adds with ads enabled
PERF_STAT_3_NOTE: Still 30+ added requests with ads and tracking settings turned off.
PERF_ROW_BUNDLE_DISQUS: 400+ KB of scripts, styles, and ads
PERF_ROW_REQUESTS_DISQUS: 90+ with ads on, 30+ with ads off
PERF_TABLE_NOTE: Disqus figures are from independent published measurements of live sites running the default Disqus configuration. FastComments script size is measured from the production build.
PERF_QUOTE_TEXT: Like their name says, they are the fastest loading comment system I've seen that isn't self-hosted. I used disqus until I found out they were tracking users...
PRIVACY_BODY_1: In 2021, Norway's Data Protection Authority issued notice of its intent to fine Disqus 25 million NOK, about 2.5 million euros, after finding it had tracked users without their knowledge or consent. The authority found that Disqus collected and shared personal data with advertising partners on sites where users never opted in.
PRIVACY_BODY_2: FastComments was designed with privacy as a core principle. Commenting is the product, not your readers' data.
PRIVACY_ROW_TRACKING_DISQUS: Tracks readers across sites to build ad profiles
PRIVACY_ROW_GDPR_FC: Compliant by design, EU data residency available
PRIVACY_ROW_GDPR_DISQUS: 2.5 million euro fine notice from Norway's DPA
PRIVACY_ROW_BANNER_DISQUS: Ad and tracking cookies require consent
PRIVACY_OUTRO: When you embed Disqus, you embed an advertising and tracking platform along with the comment section. With FastComments, commenting is all that happens on your page. No ads, no trackers, no reader data leaving your site.
PRIVACY_CALLOUT: GDPR holds you responsible for the third-party scripts you load. FastComments processes data in compliance with GDPR and offers EU data residency on eu.fastcomments.com, with primary hosting in Germany.
FEAT_BODY: FastComments is a full commenting platform with capabilities Disqus does not offer, starting with live comments that appear without a page refresh.
FEAT_ROW_REALTIME_FC: Comments appear instantly for every viewer
FEAT_ROW_WHITE_LABEL_DISQUS: Disqus branding stays on the free plan
FEAT_ROW_SDKS_DISQUS: WordPress plugin and a few others
FEAT_ROW_SSR_FC: Yes, comments render into your HTML
SEO_BODY_1: Disqus renders comments inside an iframe. Search engine crawlers do not reliably index iframe content, so the discussion on your pages contributes nothing to your rankings.
SEO_BODY_2: FastComments supports server-side rendering. Comments become part of your page's HTML before it reaches the browser, fully visible to crawlers.
SEO_OUTRO: Every comment is long-tail keyword content written by your readers. With FastComments SSR it works for your search visibility instead of sitting invisible inside an iframe.
PRICING_BODY: Disqus offers a free tier, and your readers pay for it with ads, tracking, and slower pages. When you count the real impact, free is the most expensive plan on this table.
PRICING_ROW_ADS_DISQUS_FREE: Yes, with a required top placement
PRICING_COST_HEADING: The true cost of Disqus Free
PRICING_COST_ITEM_1: Your readers see ads they did not ask for
PRICING_COST_ITEM_2: Their browsing data is collected and shared with ad networks
PRICING_COST_ITEM_3: Your pages load about 4 seconds slower
PRICING_COST_ITEM_4: Your Core Web Vitals scores drop, and rankings follow
PRICING_COST_ITEM_5: Your comment content sits invisible to search engines
PRICING_COST_ITEM_6: You carry the GDPR liability for trackers you embed
PRICING_OUTRO: FastComments Flex starts at $0.99 per month, with usage-based pricing that scales from hobby blogs to large publishers. Every plan starts with a 30-day free trial.
PRICING_TABLE_NOTE: Disqus prices as published on the Disqus pricing page, July 2026. FastComments prices from the FastComments pricing page.
MIG_BODY: FastComments ships with a built-in Disqus importer. Comments, threads, usernames, avatars, votes, and timestamps transfer automatically, and images move to the FastComments CDN. Re-running an import is safe, so you can test it as many times as you like.
MIG_STEP_1_BODY: Log into your Disqus admin panel and request an export. Disqus emails you a compressed XML file of your community.
MIG_STEP_2_BODY: Upload the export file in the FastComments dashboard under Manage Data, then Import. The importer reads the Disqus XML format natively, threading and all.
MIG_STEP_3_BODY: Replace the Disqus snippet with the FastComments snippet. On WordPress, install the FastComments plugin and deactivate Disqus.
MIG_STEP_4_BODY: Check a few pages to confirm the comments landed where they belong. Comments stay tied to your page URLs, so old discussions appear exactly where they were.
MIG_NO_LOCK_IN: No lock-in. FastComments supports full data export at any time. If you ever leave, your data leaves with you.
MIG_CTA_SUB: 30 days free. No credit card required. Import your Disqus comments during the trial.
CUSTOMERS_SUBTEXT: Organizations that switched to FastComments got faster pages, a cleaner privacy story, and readers who comment more.
FAQ_Q_IMPORT: Can I import my existing Disqus comments into FastComments?
FAQ_A_IMPORT: Yes. FastComments has a built-in Disqus import tool. Export your comments from Disqus as an XML archive, then upload it in the FastComments dashboard under Manage Data, then Import. Comments, threads, usernames, avatars, votes, and timestamps are preserved, and inline images move to the FastComments CDN.
FAQ_Q_URLS: Will switching from Disqus break my existing comment threads?
FAQ_A_URLS: No. The importer maps each Disqus thread to its page URL, so comments appear on the correct pages. For WordPress sites, the importer can match on Disqus thread identifiers instead of URLs, which handles permalink changes.
FAQ_Q_SPEED: How much faster is FastComments than Disqus?
FAQ_A_SPEED: The FastComments embed script is under 30 kilobytes and loads without blocking your page. Independent measurements show Disqus adds about 4 seconds of load time and dozens of HTTP requests per page.
FAQ_Q_ADS: Does FastComments show ads on my site?
FAQ_A_ADS: No. FastComments never displays advertisements. There is no ad-supported tier. Every plan is ad-free.
FAQ_A_TRACKING: No. FastComments sets no tracking cookies, builds no advertising profiles, and sells no data. Only the data needed to run the commenting service is processed, so you are not required to add a cookie banner for it.
FAQ_A_GDPR: Yes. FastComments supports data access and deletion requests, offers EU data residency on eu.fastcomments.com, and provides a Data Processing Agreement. In 2021, Norway's Data Protection Authority issued notice of its intent to fine Disqus 25 million NOK for tracking users without consent.
FAQ_A_REALTIME: Yes, live is the default. When someone posts, the comment appears instantly for everyone on the page without a refresh. Disqus does not offer live commenting.
FAQ_Q_SEO: Can search engines index my comments?
FAQ_A_SEO: Yes. With server-side rendering, comments become part of your page HTML and crawlers read them like any other content. Disqus keeps comments inside an iframe, which crawlers do not reliably index.
FAQ_A_PLATFORMS: FastComments integrates with 25+ platforms including WordPress, Shopify, Webflow, Wix, Ghost, Hugo, Docusaurus, Moodle, and every major JavaScript framework, plus SDKs for 10+ programming languages.
FAQ_Q_EXPORT: What if I want to leave FastComments later?
FAQ_A_EXPORT: You can export all of your data at any time. There is no lock-in; your comments belong to you.
FAQ_Q_TRIAL: Does FastComments offer a free trial?
FAQ_A_TRIAL: Yes, every plan starts with a 30-day free trial and no credit card is required. Import your Disqus comments during the trial and see the difference on your own site.
FAQ_A_SELF_HOST: Yes. Beyond the managed cloud, FastComments offers on-premises deployment on your own servers for organizations with strict data residency or compliance requirements.
FAQ_A_SPAM: A continuously trained AI classifier reviews comments, alongside keyword filtering, rate limits, and moderator tools. You can also enable AI moderation agents that read every comment with full thread context and queue the hard calls for human approval.
SOURCE_1: Marko Saric: Why you should remove Disqus from your site. Independent measurement of a live site; page load went from 1.8 to 5.7 seconds, page size from 277 KB to 707 KB, and requests from 11 to 102 after adding Disqus, with 44 requests remaining after disabling ad and tracking settings.
SOURCE_2: Datatilsynet, the Norwegian Data Protection Authority: Intent to issue 2.5 million euro fine to Disqus Inc., May 2021.
SOURCE_3: TechCrunch: Disqus facing $3M fine in Norway for tracking users without consent, May 2021.
SOURCE_4: Victor Zhou: Why I Replaced Disqus. Measured over 10x page weight and over 6x request count after adding Disqus to a page.
SOURCE_5: Google web.dev: Core Web Vitals documentation on how slow third-party scripts affect page experience metrics.
FINAL_CTA_SUB: Join <%- Number(Math.floor(usageStats.tenantCount / 100) * 100).toLocaleString() %>+ organizations that chose faster pages and reader privacy. Import your Disqus comments in minutes and see the difference on the first page load.
